LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFFS KENNY NG AND YEN CHUNG'S MOTION FOR AN ORDER GRANTING LEAVE TO FILE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS GRANTED ON THE FOLLOWING THREE CONDITIONS: 1) PLAINTIFFS MUST FILE FULL NON-EVASIVE RESPONSES TO THE FOLLOWING DEEMED- SERVED INTERROGATORIES AND DOCUMENT REQUESTS WITHIN TEN DAYS: STATE ALL FACTS THAT SUPPORT YOUR TWO NEW CLAIMS, IDENTIFY ALL PERSONS WITH KNOWLEDGE OF THOSE FACTS AND WHICH FACTS THEY HAVE KNOWLEDGE ABOUT, AND IDENTIFY AND PRODUCE ALL DOCUMENTS REFERRING TO SUCH FACTS; 2) IF WELLS FARGO SO DESIRES, PLAINTIFFS MUST STIPULATE TO A TRIAL CONTINUANCE OF NO MORE THAN THREE MONTHS; AND 3) IF THE TRIAL IS CONTINUED, PLAINTIFFS MUST AGREE THAT WELLS FARGO MAY FILE A MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION ON THE TWO NEW CLAIMS ON 30 CALENDAR DAYS' NOTICE WITH OPPOSITION PAPERS DUE 14 CALENDAR DAYS BEFORE THE HEARING AND REPLY PAPERS DUE 5 CALENDAR DAYS BEFORE THE HEARING. WITH THESE CONDITIONS, WELLS FARGO SUFFERS NO PREJUDICE FROM THE GRANTING OF THE MOTION AND THE MOTION IS FAVORED BY THE STRONG CALIFORNIA POLICIES OF ALLOWING AMENDMENTS TO PLEADINGS AND DECIDING CASES ON THEIR MERITS. COURTS ARE BOUND TO APPLY A POLICY OF GREAT LIBERALITY IN PERMITTING AMENDMENTS TO THE COMPLAINT AT ANY STAGE OF THE PROCEEDINGS, UP TO AND INCLUDING TRIAL. (MESLER V. BRAGG MANAGEMENT CO. (1985) 39 CAL.3D 290, 296-297). ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976.

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT WELLS FARGO BANK'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, ALTERNATIVELY SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED IN ITS ENTIRETY. SEE MINUTES AND/OR TRANSCRIPT FOR DETAILS. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976.

Motion For An Order Granting Leave To File First Amended Complaint
Off Calendar May-24-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The Court confirmed its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFFS KENNY NG and YEN CHUNG'S Motion For An Order Granting Leave To File First Amended Complaint is granted on the following three conditions: 1) plaintiffs must file full non-evasive responses to the following deemed- served interrogatories and document requests within ten days: state all facts that support your two new claims, identify all persons with knowledge of those facts and which facts they have knowledge about, and identify and produce all documents referring to such facts; 2) if Wells Fargo so desires, plaintiffs must stipulate to a trial continuance of no more than three months; and 3) if the trial is continued, plaintiffs must agree that Wells Fargo may file a motion for summary adjudication on the two new claims on 30 calendar days' notice with opposition papers due 14 calendar days before the hearing and reply papers due 5 calendar days before the hearing. With these conditions, Wells Fargo suffers no prejudice from the granting of the motion and the motion is favored by the strong California policies of allowing amendments to pleadings and deciding cases on their merits. Courts are bound to apply a policy of great liberality in permitting amendments to the complaint at any stage of the proceedings, up to and including trial. (Mesler v. Bragg Management Co. (1985) 39 Cal.3d 290, 296-297). Order signed.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , Or Alternatively Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar May-24-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The Court confirmed its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT WELLS FARGO BANK'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, Alternatively Summary Adjudication is denied in its entirety. Assuming without deciding that plaintiffs' first and second causes of action are governed by a two year limitations period, there is a triable issue whether plaintiffs knew or should have known more than two years prior to their filing of this case that Wells Fargo did not permanently modify their loan in the way they contend that Wells Fargo represented that it would. (Plaintiffs' Additional Facts 14-18, 20-21). Order signed.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , Or Alternatively Summary Adjudication

DEFENDANT WELLS FARGO BANK'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, Or Alternatively Summary Adjudication is continued to May 24, 2016 at 9:30 a.m., in Dept. 302 to be heard concurrently with plaintiff's motion to file a first amended complaint.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)
